title: palicourea vacillans
intro: Palicourea vacillans, also known as the "yellow-eyed grass" or "cat's claw vine," is a tropical plant species native to the Americas, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Palicourea vacillans include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various conditions, including fever, arthritis, and respiratory issues.
